w:r.w:t: Everything a new chat needs to continue building carousels from Day 43 onwards.
Aspiring Full Stack / AI Developer. Documents learning publicly on Instagram.
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Git, Firebase, OOP, ReactJS, Python, NodeJS
A repeatable, fast system for generating polished Instagram carousels that document Abdullah's #100DaysOfCode journey. Claude's job is to:
Analyse the day's learning material (lesson screenshots + GitHub code)
Decide how to frame it — as an informational educational post, not just a learning log
Write all slide content, keeping it concise and social-media appropriate
Generate a fully self-contained HTML file the user downloads and exports
— one post per day, documenting what was learned and built. Content from lesson screenshots and GitHub code.
— occasional multi-day synthesis. One clean educational carousel on a single concept (e.g. 'DOM Manipulation explained'). Designed for reach and shareability.

turn learning into teaching. Reframe what was learned that day as something useful for the reader. See Day 40 example: 'Building a Google Keep Clone' with 'Key Concept: map() + innerHTML' instead of just listing topics covered.
Day 42 onwards uses a terminal / hacker aesthetic. Previous days used an orange brand system — that system is superseded by this one.
#00FF41 — Matrix green. Main accent: prompt dollar, borders, highlights
#60DCFC — React cyan. All React-related highlights and accents
#00B82F — Darker green for light slide borders
rgba(255,255,255,0.35) — Muted text on dark slides
rgba(0,255,65,0.18) — Green border on dark slides
#007A20 — Green for prompts and tags on light slides
#141210 — Near-black body text on light slides
JetBrains Mono exclusively throughout — no other fonts. This reinforces the terminal / hacker aesthetic.
Terminal commands as slide prompts: ~/day42 $ git log --oneline
Status tags in bracket notation: [ COMPLETE ] JAVASCRIPT GOOGLE KEEP
Comment lines for subtext: // vanilla JS era: done. React era: begins.
Cover taglines in all-caps bracket notation: [ # 100DAYSOFCODE ] / [ DAY 42 ]
Concise and technical. Every word earns its place.
Each slide: .slide wrapper, full 480x640 with absolute-positioned content
~1.27 MB with three base64-embedded images (DP + two logos)
4.2 Asset Strategy — Critical for file:// Export

All images on slides that will be exported MUST be base64 data URIs embedded directly in the HTML src attribute. If any image is a file path (e.g. insta-dp.jpg), the browser cannot fetch it under file://, the canvas becomes tainted, and toDataURL() throws a SecurityError.
React atom hero — draw on offscreen canvas on page load, assign as data URL to img#react-hero-img
CTA gradient — draw on offscreen canvas on page load, assign as data URL to img#cta-bg-img
Videos — referenced as relative path (js-keep-video.mov). Cannot be base64 in HTML. For file:// export, skip video slides or use a local HTTP server.
A script tag with a src attribute CANNOT also contain inline JavaScript. Browsers silently ignore the inline body. Always split into two separate tags:
The Day 42 carousel uses shared helper functions for clean PNG export:
Clones the slide, injects animation:none style, detects .cta-bg for gradient bg string (getComputedStyle returns transparent for gradients). Returns {clone, bg}.
Passed as html2canvas onclone callback. Strips filter:drop-shadow from the .react-hero img on referenceElement (the cloned root). Must target referenceElement, not just documentClone.
Wraps the clone in a 480x640 off-screen div (no z-index:-1 — that can hide from html2canvas), runs html2canvas at 2.25x scale, returns PNG data URL.
For http:// served pages only: fetches each img src, converts to base64 data URL via FileReader, replaces src in-place before capture. Does NOT fix file:// paths — use embedded data URLs instead.

The React atom is drawn on a 4x internal canvas (800x800 with ctx.scale(4,4)), but styled at 360px CSS. This ensures crisp edges at 2.25x export scale. The filter:drop-shadow CSS must be stripped from the clone before capture (it can cause html2canvas to cut off the glow area).
pointerdown / pointermove / pointerup on .vp — 50px threshold to trigger slide change
Row of dots below carousel — active dot highlighted in green
Every carousel is assembled from this menu of slide types. When given new content, choose the types that best fit what was learned or built. Mix and match freely — there is no fixed sequence.
Hook. Day number, topic title, profile photo, logos. Always slide 1.
DP + terminal prompt row, cover taglines ([ # 100DAYSOFCODE ] / [ DAY XX ]), big heading, logo icons top-right, React/project hero graphic
white-space: nowrap on .tagline to prevent line breaks. .cover-taglines must be a direct child of .sp-cover (not inside .content which has max-width: 240px).
Show the running project. Screenshot or video of the actual app UI.
Terminal prompt, status tag, small heading, screenshot img or video element, comment line, repo info bar
Images: max-width:100%; height:auto; object-fit:contain; max-height:320px to fit within slide. Videos: remove background:#000 from video element to avoid letterbox borders.
What was built — list of 3-5 features or capabilities.
Terminal prompt, status tag, heading, feature rows (icon + title + description)
Each feature row: icon box + bold title + muted description. Green borders on dark, cyan borders for React variant.
Teach a concept. Syntax-highlighted code snippet + plain-English explanation.
Prompt, heading, carbon-style code block, callout explanation box
Comments always on their own line — never inline with code on the same span. Inline code spans (.c / .cd) applied to all method/property mentions in explanations.
Numbered steps, learning milestones, or data flow explanation.
Prompt, status tag, heading, numbered step rows (number + title + description)
React variant (.lt.rlt) swaps accent color to cyan (#0284c7) for numbers and progress bar fill.
Show challenges completed or skills being practiced.
Prompt, status tag, heading, challenge rows similar to feature rows
Can be combined with or replaced by a code block slide if a specific challenge concept is worth explaining in detail.
Call to action. Follow prompt, handle, day badge. Always last slide.
Gradient (.cta-bg) — radial green glow from bottom
Terminal prompt, status tag, big heading (N days in. Still building.), comment line with cursor blink, follow card with handle + tags
Gradient must be pre-rendered to canvas on page load and injected as img#cta-bg-img for reliable export. getComputedStyle returns transparent for gradient elements.
Cover + Project Visual + Feature List + CTA (4 slides, compact)
Cover + Visual + Features + Progress + Screenshot + CTA (6 slides, Day 42 pattern)
Cover + Code Block x2-3 + Step/Progress + Challenges + CTA (6-7 slides)
Cover + Code Block + CTA (3 slides, minimal)
Cover + Step x3 + Code Block x2 + CTA (7 slides, educational)
Light slide — #F5F5EF background, dark text, green accents
React light variant — accent color overridden to React cyan (#0284c7)
Gradient CTA — radial green glow. Pair with img#cta-bg-img for export.
Main slide content container — flex column, full height, 30px padding
Corner bracket marks — absolute positioned decorative corners
Profile photo + prompt row on cover slide
Circular profile photo with green border
Tagline block — must be direct child of .sp-cover, not inside .content
Status tag — 10px, green, letter-spacing, ALL CAPS
Info bar — repo link at bottom of slide
Progress fill — width set per slide as percentage
Swipe arrow — right edge chevron indicator
Flex row containing all 6 individual download buttons
Day 40: Building a Google Keep Clone
Google Keep clone — Vanilla JS, OOP (Note + App classes), full DOM manipulation
to close modal on outside click
Body-level event delegation — one listener on
Google Keep clone upgraded — notes now survive page refresh
Same repo as Day 40 — app.js was the main file updated
in one call, invoked after every mutation
state property to track which note is being edited in the modal
Graceful Modal Closure — open/close via separate buttons,
Day 42: Completing the JS Google Keep Clone + Starting React
Milestone post — vanilla JS era complete, React era begins
JS Google Keep Clone (completed) + React Google Keep (started)
6-slide terminal aesthetic — diverges from orange brand system
JS Keep Clone — what was completed:
Full vanilla JS + OOP Google Keep clone with persistent notes
localStorage persistence from Day 41 carried forward
Visual update slide showing the running app screenshot
Began rebuilding the Google Keep clone in React + Vite + JSX
npm run dev workflow, component structure started
Progress: app scaffolded, components folder created
8. Workflow — How to Create a New Carousel
Course lesson screenshots (what topics/lessons were covered that day)
Code files (GitHub link or pasted directly — app.js, challenges, etc.)
Any specific framing preferences or angles he wants to highlight
Analyse all material. Ask: what is the most interesting or teachable concept from today? Frame that as the carousel theme. Options:
Single project deep-dive — "Building X" with key concepts shown via code blocks
Concept explainer — "How [X] works" / "N Ways to [do X]"
Data flow / mental model — numbered steps showing a process
Before/after — what changed, what was added, why it matters
Always turn learning into teaching. The reader should gain something from each slide, not just watch a learning log.
Produce a complete standalone HTML file using the Day 41 file as the template. Key checklist:
Fonts imported from Google Fonts (Bricolage Grotesque, DM Sans, JetBrains Mono)
Sizer div pattern used (NOT aspect-ratio CSS) — see Section 4.2
All image slots use dashed placeholder divs with
Footer on every slide except slide 1 with correct day number
Progress bar percentages correct for total slide count
Swipe arrow absent from last slide
Instructions box at top of page body
Download button label: "Download all [N] slides as PNG"
Wait 2–3 seconds for Google Fonts to load
Click orange download button — all slides export automatically
Profile pic: Instagram photo URL or local image
JS logo: can use uploaded file or public CDN URL
Upload 7 PNG files to Instagram as a carousel post
Use the suggested caption from Claude
aspect-ratio CSS causes subpixel bleed in html2canvas
Use padding-top: 125% sizer div pattern. Capture .slide-inner not .vp. Hard-crop output canvas.
Fonts look wrong in downloaded PNG
Google Fonts not loaded before capture
Wait 2–3 seconds after opening file. Fonts must load before clicking download.
Code block comments on wrong line
Inline comments after code share the .cl span
Comments ALWAYS get their own separate <span class="cl"> line. Never mix comment and code on same line.
CDN URL has expiry token (oe=... parameter)
Re-fetch URL from Instagram or host image on stable CDN. Placeholder is safe default.
Some browsers require user gesture for each download
300ms delay between downloads handles this in most cases. Chrome works best.

file:// + non-base64 image src on any slide. Canvas is tainted; toDataURL throws. Fix: embed ALL exported slide images as base64 data URIs directly in HTML. fetch()-based inlining does NOT work under file://.
Canvas was 200x200 scaled to 360px CSS. Fix: use 800x800 internal canvas with ctx.scale(4,4) so pixel density matches the 2.25x export scale.
html2canvas clips the capture area excluding glow. Fix: strip filter property in onclone callback — target referenceElement (the cloned root), not just documentClone.
Can cause html2canvas to miss elements depending on stacking context. Fix: use left:-10000px instead of z-index:-1 to hide the off-screen wrapper.

toDataURL threw on slide 1 due to tainted canvas; catch only logged the error so day42_slide_01.png was never written while other slides continued. Fix: embed base64 images (above) + surface per-slide errors with alert.
Regex captured wrong group when inserting base64 data URLs, dropping the opening tag markup. Fix: always write the full replacement string including the tag prefix, not just the src value.
Taglines inside .content (max-width:240px) wrapped. Fix: move .cover-taglines to direct child of .sp-cover and add white-space:nowrap on .tagline.
Gradient slide background (orange gradient, CTA only)
Section label tag — dark slide (light orange)
Slide heading — light / dark / gradient variant
Inline code span — light slides (orange on cream bg)
Inline code span — dark slides (light orange on dark bg)
Feature row — dark slide (icon box + title + description)
Step row — light slide (number + title + description)
Slide footer — flex row, handle left, day badge right
Code block — container + header + filename label
CTA logo lockup — row + circle + name
Dot navigation — row + dot + active dot
Report v4 — @uncledullaz Instagram Carousel System. Full terminal design system, unified slide library, export fixes.
